title: The adapter/src/channel/ directory holds all Swarm-sync integration code, split into factory wiring, the IChannel implementation, and manifest management.
content:
## `adapter/src/channel/` layout

```
adapter/src/channel/
  index.ts                    - Public exports
  composite-factory.ts        - Routes "gql"/"swarm" to sub-factories
  swarm-channel.ts            - IChannel: outbox push + inbox pull
  swarm-channel-factory.ts    - Creates SwarmChannel from ChannelConfig
  create-composite-factory.ts - createSwarmSyncBuilder() for ReactorBuilder.withSync()
  register-swarm-channel.ts   - Runtime factory injection (legacy/edge cases)
  add-swarm-remote.ts         - Per-drive Swarm remote registration
  manifest-manager.ts         - User + drive manifest writes
```

### How they fit

- `swarm-channel.ts` is the new code path — a native `IChannel` implementation that SyncManager treats identically to the GQL channel.
- `composite-factory.ts` plus `create-composite-factory.ts` form the wiring that lets GQL and Swarm coexist behind one factory.
- `add-swarm-remote.ts` is the entry point used by plugin init / registration retry to attach a Swarm remote to a specific drive.
- `manifest-manager.ts` is the only module that writes the user and drive manifest feeds, keeping manifest-write logic in one place.
- `register-swarm-channel.ts` exists for edge cases where the factory needs to be injected after reactor construction (legacy paths).
description: Key files: swarm-channel.ts (IChannel), composite-factory.ts (routing), create-composite-factory.ts (builder wiring), add-swarm-remote.ts, manifest-manager.ts.
